topic: cool service temperature for sparkling wine

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Sparkling wine contains dissolved carbon dioxide and is prepared for service.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cool temperatures retain dissolved carbon dioxide, reduce foaming and preserve freshness, while excessive cold can suppress aroma.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Dry sparkling wine is normally served well chilled, with exact recommendations varying by style and complexity.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: cool service controls pressure and freshness, but more complex sparkling wines may be served slightly warmer.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
